title: astripomoea hyoscyamoides var. hyoscyamoides
intro: Astripomoea hyoscyamoides var. hyoscyamoides is a plant species, also known as the 'annual devil's cucumber', that belongs to the family Convolvulaceae. The plant contains medicinal properties, including hyoscyamine, a tropane alkaloid with antispasmodic and sedative effects. The health benefits of this plant include relief from anxiety, insomnia, and muscle spasms, as well as potential applications in treating Parkinson's disease and glaucoma. Astripomoea hyoscyamoides var. hyoscyamoides can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusion, decoction, or tincture, although the plant is typically used under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional due to its potential side effects.
